subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
Isle of Cats includes:

- **1 island board** (the Isle of Cats) with a fish track and discovery area
- **4 boat boards** (one per player) with a polyomino grid and rooms
- **85 cat tiles** in 5 colors (blue, green, red, purple, orange) — polyomino shapes of various sizes (1–5 squares)
- **5 Oshax tiles** (wild cats)
- **150 discovery cards** (rescue cards, lesson cards, treasure cards, and Oshax cards)
- **20 common treasure tiles** and **10 rare treasure tiles**
- **Fish tokens** (currency)
- **Boot tokens** (for speed/turn order)
- **1 Vesh Darkhand** figure (the villain approaching the island)
- **Day tracker** and round markers

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Place the **island board** in the center of the table.
2. Set the **day tracker** to Day 1 (the game lasts 5 days/rounds).
3. Fill the island with **cat tiles** based on the round: draw cats from the bag and place them in the fields on the island board.
4. Place **fish tokens** and **boot tokens** in supply piles.
5. Shuffle the **discovery card deck**.
6. Place **treasure tiles** in their designated supply areas.
7. Place the **Vesh Darkhand** figure on the day track to mark the approaching threat.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
1. Each player takes a **boat board** — this is your personal grid where you'll place rescued cats.
2. Your boat has **7 rooms** divided by walls. Uncovered rooms lose points at game end.
3. Each player starts with **20 fish** (the game's currency).
4. Deal **7 discovery cards** to each player.
5. Players also receive starting **boot tokens** for turn order.
6. In the solo mode, special rules adjust the card draft and cat availability.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
In Isle of Cats, you are rescuing cats from a mysterious island before the evil Vesh Darkhand arrives. Cats come as **polyomino tiles** (Tetris-like shapes) that you must fit onto your boat's grid.

Over **5 rounds** (days), you draft cards, spend fish to rescue cats, and arrange them on your boat. You score points for:
- **Cat families** (groups of adjacent same-colored cats)
- **Covering rooms** on your boat (uncovered rooms lose 5 points each)
- **Rare treasures** collected
- **Lesson cards** (secret and public objectives)

The player with the most points after 5 days wins.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each day (round) follows these phases:

### Phase 1: Fishing
Draw fish tokens based on the current day (all players gain the same base amount plus bonuses from cards).

### Phase 2: Explore (Card Draft)
Deal **7 cards** to each player. Draft cards using a **two-at-a-time** method:
1. Choose **2 cards** from your hand to keep.
2. Pass the rest to the next player.
3. Repeat until all cards are drafted.

Cards cost **fish to keep** — each card has a fish cost printed on it. You must pay the cost now or discard the card.

### Phase 3: Read Lessons
Reveal any **public lesson cards** played this round. These create shared scoring objectives for all players.

### Phase 4: Rescue Cats
In turn order, players take turns selecting **cat tiles** from the island and placing them on their boats:
1. Play a **rescue card** from your hand (each has a speed/basket value).
2. Select a cat from the island whose **color matches** the rescue card (or use a wild).
3. **Place the cat tile** on your boat grid. It must fit entirely within the grid and cannot overlap other tiles.
4. Permanent baskets from previous rounds can be reused.

Each player can rescue **up to 2 cats per round** (one per rescue card pair).

### Phase 5: Rare Finds
Certain cards grant access to **rare treasures** — small tiles that fill single spaces on your boat.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Rescue Cards
Rescue cards come in pairs and determine:
- **Speed** (turn order priority)
- **Basket color** (which color cat you can rescue)
- Playing 2 rescue cards lets you take 1 cat of the matching color.

### Placing Cat Tiles
- Cat tiles are **polyomino shapes** (like Tetris pieces) of varying sizes.
- Tiles must be placed **entirely within your boat grid**.
- Tiles **cannot overlap** each other.
- Larger cats are harder to fit but cover more room space.
- **Oshax** (wild cats) can count as any color for family scoring.

### Lesson Cards
- **Public lessons**: Revealed for all players. Score conditions like "3 VP per row with 3+ cats."
- **Private lessons**: Kept secret, scored at game end. Personal objectives like "5 VP if you have cats of all 5 colors."

### Treasure Tiles
- **Common treasures**: Small tiles (1–2 squares) that help fill gaps.
- **Rare treasures**: Earned through specific cards. Worth VP and fill spaces.

### Permanent Baskets
Some cards give you **permanent baskets** — these persist between rounds, letting you rescue cats without needing rescue card pairs.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Cat Families
A **family** is a group of **3 or more adjacent same-colored cats** (orthogonally connected, not diagonal). Families score bonus points based on size.

### Oshax (Wild Cats)
Oshax tiles are **wild** — when scoring families, an Oshax adjacent to a color group counts as that color. If adjacent to multiple colors, you choose which family it joins.

### Rooms
Your boat has **7 rooms** separated by walls. At game end, any room that is **not completely filled** with tiles costs you **-5 points**. Filling all rooms is critical.

### Speed and Turn Order
Rescue cards have speed values. Higher speed goes first when choosing cats from the island. Going first gives better cat selection.

### Solo Mode
Isle of Cats includes a dedicated **solo mode** where you play against a simulated opponent (Sister and Brother rules) with a simplified card mechanism.

### Family Mode
A simplified variant removes the card drafting and uses a basic draw-and-play mechanism, suitable for younger players.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
After **5 days** (rounds), calculate final scores:

### Positive Scoring
1. **Cat families**: Score based on family size:
   - 3 cats: **8 points**
   - 4 cats: **11 points**
   - 5 cats: **15 points**
   - 6+ cats: **20 points**
2. **Rare treasures**: Each rare treasure tile is worth its printed VP value.
3. **Public lesson cards**: Score based on the shared objectives.
4. **Private lesson cards**: Score based on your secret objectives.

### Negative Scoring
5. **Uncovered rooms**: Each room on your boat that is **not completely filled** with tiles costs **-5 points**.
6. **Rats**: Any visible rat symbols (printed on the boat, not covered by tiles) cost **-1 point each**.

### Winning
The player with the **highest total score** wins. Tiebreaker: most cat tiles on your boat.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Fill your rooms**: Each uncovered room is -5 points. Prioritize covering all 7 rooms over building huge families.
- **Plan your grid**: Before drafting, visualize where tiles will go. Corners and edges are hardest to fill.
- **Balance fish spending**: Cards cost fish to keep, but fish are limited. Don't over-draft expensive cards.
- **Color focus**: Focus on 2–3 cat colors for families rather than spreading across all 5.
- **Small tiles are valuable**: Small cat tiles (1–2 squares) are easier to fit in gaps and still contribute to families.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Room coverage first**: Always ensure you can fill all rooms. The -5 penalty per room adds up fast. Use treasures and small cats to plug gaps.
- **Big family focus**: A 6+ cat family scores 20 points — nearly as much as 4 covered rooms. Build one or two large families of the same color.
- **Lesson card synergy**: Draft private lessons that match your natural playstyle (e.g., color focus or room coverage). Lessons worth 10+ points are game-changers.
- **Speed priority**: In early rounds, draft high-speed rescue cards to get first pick of the best cat tiles.
- **Oshax flexibility**: Oshax wild cats are extremely valuable. They bridge two color groups or extend a family. Prioritize getting them.
- **Edge-in placement**: Place cats from the edges of your boat inward. This avoids creating unreachable gaps in the center.
- **Permanent baskets**: Cards that give permanent baskets provide value every round. Draft them early for maximum benefit.
